channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump's name must come off of the Kennedy Center, judge rules
item.description: The judge wrote in his 94-page ruling that it was "crystal clear" that the arts complex was named for the late president John F. Kennedy. He also ruled that the center could not wind down its programming and close for two years of renovations – at least for now
item.content:encoded:
added to the Kennedy Center in December of 2025. This photo was taken in April 2026.'/>

The judge wrote in his 94-page ruling that it was "crystal clear" that the arts complex was named for the late president John F. Kennedy. He also ruled that the center could not wind down its programming and close for two years of renovations – at least for now

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: Why are people attacking Ebola clinics? It revolves around trust, death and body bags
item.description: In echoes of past outbreaks, community members are attacking clinics, distrusting doctors and following burial traditions that could lead to more cases of Ebola.
item.content:encoded:
In echoes of past outbreaks, community members are attacking clinics, distrusting doctors and following burial traditions that could lead to more cases of Ebola.

(Image credit: Gradel Muyisa Mumbere)
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: Louisiana lawmakers pass a congressional map to dismantle a majority-Black district
item.description: Louisiana's Republican lawmakers raced to eliminate one of two majority-Black congressional seats in the state after the U.S. Supreme Court ruled the current map unconstitutional in a sweeping ruling.
item.content:encoded:
Louisiana's Republican lawmakers raced to eliminate one of two majority-Black congressional seats in the state after the U.S. Supreme Court ruled the current map unconstitutional in a sweeping ruling.

(Image credit: Michael DeMocker)
item.dc:creator: Sam Gringlas

item.title: Here's how we're coping with high gas prices, according to Costco and Walmart
item.description: Sky-high gas prices have drivers going out of their way for discounts at the pump. Oil executives warn that even higher prices might be on the horizon.
item.content:encoded:
Sky-high gas prices have drivers going out of their way for discounts at the pump. Oil executives warn that even higher prices might be on the horizon.

(Image credit: Paul Sancya)
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: Asia defense summit opens amid doubts over U.S. priorities
item.description: The Shangri-La Dialogue, hosted by the International Institute for Strategic Studies, will also address tensions in the Middle East and Russia's war on Ukraine.
item.content:encoded:
The Shangri-La Dialogue, hosted by the International Institute for Strategic Studies, will also address tensions in the Middle East and Russia's war on Ukraine.

(Image credit: Achmad Ibrahim)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Israel, Lebanon officials to meet. And, judge won't block Trump's mail-in voting order
item.description: Israel and Lebanon officials are set to meet today as U.S.-Iran peace talks continue. And, a federal judge declined to block Trump's executive order restricting mail-in voting.
item.content:encoded:
Israel and Lebanon officials are set to meet today as U.S.-Iran peace talks continue. And, a federal judge declined to block Trump's executive order restricting mail-in voting.

(Image credit: Kawant Haju)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Meteorologists were central to D-Day. 'Pressure' tells the story of navigating uncertainty
item.description: The new movie, based on writer and actor David Haig's 2014 play, dramatizes the tensions between military leaders and meteorologists in the lead up to the Allied invasion of Normandy.
item.content:encoded:
The new movie, based on writer and actor David Haig's 2014 play, dramatizes the tensions between military leaders and meteorologists in the lead up to the Allied invasion of Normandy.

(Image credit: Alex Bailey)
item.dc:creator: Daniel Jonah Wolpert

item.title: Photos show the world's 'uncounted' people -- and what it takes to be counted
item.description: Colombian-American photographer and filmmaker Juan Arredondo turns his lens on the people of the world who do not have birth and death certificates — and how these vital records are created.
item.content:encoded:
Colombian-American photographer and filmmaker Juan Arredondo turns his lens on the people of the world who do not have birth and death certificates — and how these vital records are created.

(Image credit: Juan Arredondo)
item.dc:creator: Diane Cole

item.title: Russian drone launched against Ukraine crashes in Romania, injuring 2
item.description: A Russian drone that was part of an overnight attack on Ukraine crashed into an apartment building in eastern Romania, injuring two people, Romanian authorities said Friday.
item.content:encoded:
A Russian drone that was part of an overnight attack on Ukraine crashed into an apartment building in eastern Romania, injuring two people, Romanian authorities said Friday.

(Image credit: via AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: WHO chief lands in Congo to address rare Ebola outbreak amid distrust and insecurity
item.description: The head of the World Health Organization arrived in Congo's capital, Kinshasa, to witness efforts against an outbreak of a rare type of Ebola virus.
item.content:encoded:
The head of the World Health Organization arrived in Congo's capital, Kinshasa, to witness efforts against an outbreak of a rare type of Ebola virus.

(Image credit: Samy Ntumba Shambuyi)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
